channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Emergence of a Fluctuating Ground State in Y-Kapellasite under Pressure
item.description:
Author(s): Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal

Hydrostatic pressure drives a magnetically ordered kagome material into a fluctuating ground state that shares key features with quantum spin liquids.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136701] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal

Hydrostatic pressure drives a magnetically ordered kagome material into a fluctuating ground state that shares key features with quantum spin liquids.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136701] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergence of a Fluctuating Ground State in Y-Kapellasite under Pressure
item.dc:creator: Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Element-Selective Probing of Ultrafast Ferromagnetic-Antiferromagnetic Order Dynamics in Fe/CoO Bilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Chowdhury S. Awsaf, Sangeeta Thakur, Markus Weißenhofer, Jendrik Gördes, Marcel Walter, Mohamad-Assaad Mawass, Niko Pontius, Christian Schüßler-Langeheine, Peter M. Oppeneer, and Wolfgang Kuch

The ultrafast magnetization dynamics of an epitaxial $\mathrm{Fe}/\mathrm{CoO}$ bilayer on Ag(001) is examined in an element-resolved way by resonant soft-x-ray reflectivity. The transient magnetic linear dichroism at the Co ${L}_{2}$ edge and the magnetic circular dichroism at the Fe ${L}_{3}$ edge…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126705] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chowdhury S. Awsaf, Sangeeta Thakur, Markus Weißenhofer, Jendrik Gördes, Marcel Walter, Mohamad-Assaad Mawass, Niko Pontius, Christian Schüßler-Langeheine, Peter M. Oppeneer, and Wolfgang Kuch

The ultrafast magnetization dynamics of an epitaxial Fe/CoO bilayer on Ag(001) is examined in an element-resolved way by resonant soft-x-ray reflectivity. The transient magnetic linear dichroism at the Co L2 edge and the magnetic circular dichroism at the Fe L3 edge measured in reflection in a pump–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126705] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Element-Selective Probing of Ultrafast Ferromagnetic-Antiferromagnetic Order Dynamics in Fe/CoO Bilayers
item.dc:creator: Chowdhury S. Awsaf, Sangeeta Thakur, Markus Weißenhofer, Jendrik Gördes, Marcel Walter, Mohamad-Assaad Mawass, Niko Pontius, Christian Schüßler-Langeheine, Peter M. Oppeneer, and Wolfgang Kuch
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intrinsic Heralding and Optimal Decoders for Non-Abelian Topological Order
item.description:
Author(s): Dian Jing, Pablo Sala, Liang Jiang, and Ruben Verresen

Topological order (TO) provides a natural platform for storing and manipulating quantum information. However, its stability to noise has only been systematically understood for Abelian TOs. In this Letter, we exploit the nondeterministic fusion of non-Abelian anyons to inform active error-correction…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120405] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dian Jing, Pablo Sala, Liang Jiang, and Ruben Verresen

Topological order (TO) provides a natural platform for storing and manipulating quantum information. However, its stability to noise has only been systematically understood for Abelian TOs. In this Letter, we exploit the nondeterministic fusion of non-Abelian anyons to inform active error-correction…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120405] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Intrinsic Heralding and Optimal Decoders for Non-Abelian Topological Order
item.dc:creator: Dian Jing, Pablo Sala, Liang Jiang, and Ruben Verresen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Light-Induced Odd-Parity Magnetism in Conventional Antiferromagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): Shengpu Huang, Zheng Qin, Fangyang Zhan, Dong-Hui Xu, Da-Shuai Ma, and Rui Wang

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126703] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shengpu Huang, Zheng Qin, Fangyang Zhan, Dong-Hui Xu, Da-Shuai Ma, and Rui Wang

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126703] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Light-Induced Odd-Parity Magnetism in Conventional Antiferromagnetism
item.dc:creator: Shengpu Huang, Zheng Qin, Fangyang Zhan, Dong-Hui Xu, Da-Shuai Ma, and Rui Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Floquet Odd-Parity Collinear Magnets
item.description:
Author(s): Tongshuai Zhu, Di Zhou, Huaiqiang Wang, Su-Huai Wei, and Jiawei Ruan

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126704] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tongshuai Zhu, Di Zhou, Huaiqiang Wang, Su-Huai Wei, and Jiawei Ruan

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126704] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet Odd-Parity Collinear Magnets
item.dc:creator: Tongshuai Zhu, Di Zhou, Huaiqiang Wang, Su-Huai Wei, and Jiawei Ruan

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Loop Current Order on the Kagome Lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Jun Zhan, Hendrik Hohmann, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ruiqing Fu, Sen Zhou, Ziqiang Wang, Ronny Thomale, Xianxin Wu, and Jiangping Hu

Recent discoveries in kagome materials have unveiled their capacity to harbor exotic quantum states, including intriguing charge density wave (CDW) and superconductivity. Notably, accumulating experimental evidence suggests time-reversal symmetry breaking within the CDW, hinting at the long-pursued …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jun Zhan, Hendrik Hohmann, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ruiqing Fu, Sen Zhou, Ziqiang Wang, Ronny Thomale, Xianxin Wu, and Jiangping Hu

Recent discoveries in kagome materials have unveiled their capacity to harbor exotic quantum states, including intriguing charge density wave (CDW) and superconductivity. Notably, accumulating experimental evidence suggests time-reversal symmetry breaking within the CDW, hinting at the long-pursued …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Loop Current Order on the Kagome Lattice
item.dc:creator: Jun Zhan, Hendrik Hohmann, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ruiqing Fu, Sen Zhou, Ziqiang Wang, Ronny Thomale, Xianxin Wu, and Jiangping Hu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intertwined Swirling Polarization States in ${\mathrm{BaTiO}}_{3}$ with Embedded ${\mathrm{BaZrO}}_{3}$ Nanoregions
item.description:
Author(s): R. Machado, F. Di Rino, M. Sepliarsky, and M. G. Stachiotti

Ferroelectric materials embedded with dielectric inclusions offer a unique platform for exploring novel topological polar textures. Using first-principles-based atomistic simulations, we investigate the polarization behavior of a ${\mathrm{BaTiO}}_{3}$ matrix containing segregated ${\mathrm{BaZrO}}_…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126201] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. Machado, F. Di Rino, M. Sepliarsky, and M. G. Stachiotti

Ferroelectric materials embedded with dielectric inclusions offer a unique platform for exploring novel topological polar textures. Using first-principles-based atomistic simulations, we investigate the polarization behavior of a BaTiO3 matrix containing segregated BaZrO3 nanoregions. We demonstrate…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126201] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Intertwined Swirling Polarization States in ${\mathrm{BaTiO}}_{3}$ with Embedded ${\mathrm{BaZrO}}_{3}$ Nanoregions
item.dc:creator: R. Machado, F. Di Rino, M. Sepliarsky, and M. G. Stachiotti
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Orbital-Dependent Coulomb Drag in Electron-Hole Bilayer Graphene Heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Zuocheng Zhang, Ruishi Qi, Jingxu Xie, Qize Li, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Michael F. Crommie, and Feng Wang

Strong Coulomb drag is observed between Landau levels with nonzero orbital quantum numbers in a graphene heterostructure device consisting of an electron bilayer adjacent to a hole bilayer.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126303] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zuocheng Zhang, Ruishi Qi, Jingxu Xie, Qize Li, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Michael F. Crommie, and Feng Wang

Strong Coulomb drag is observed between Landau levels with nonzero orbital quantum numbers in a graphene heterostructure device consisting of an electron bilayer adjacent to a hole bilayer.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126303] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital-Dependent Coulomb Drag in Electron-Hole Bilayer Graphene Heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Zuocheng Zhang, Ruishi Qi, Jingxu Xie, Qize Li, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Michael F. Crommie, and Feng Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Subthermal Mean Transverse Energies Induced by Electron Refraction on the Jump in Mass at the Surface of Multialkali Photocathodes
item.description:
Author(s): S. A. Rozhkov, V. V. Bakin, H. E. Scheibler, V. S. Rusetsky, D. V. Gorshkov, D. A. Kustov, V. A. Golyashov, V. L. Alperovich, and O. E. Tereshchenko

The search for photocathode materials with low mean transverse energies (MTEs) and, hence, low intrinsic emittance is of crucial importance for various fields of particle and solid-state physics. Here, we demonstrate that polycrystalline multialkali ${\mathrm{Na}}_{2}\mathrm{KSb}(\mathrm{Cs},\mathrm…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 127001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. A. Rozhkov, V. V. Bakin, H. E. Scheibler, V. S. Rusetsky, D. V. Gorshkov, D. A. Kustov, V. A. Golyashov, V. L. Alperovich, and O. E. Tereshchenko

The search for photocathode materials with low mean transverse energies (MTEs) and, hence, low intrinsic emittance is of crucial importance for various fields of particle and solid-state physics. Here, we demonstrate that polycrystalline multialkali Na2KSb(Cs,Sb) photocathodes with negative effectiv…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 127001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Subthermal Mean Transverse Energies Induced by Electron Refraction on the Jump in Mass at the Surface of Multialkali Photocathodes
item.dc:creator: S. A. Rozhkov, V. V. Bakin, H. E. Scheibler, V. S. Rusetsky, D. V. Gorshkov, D. A. Kustov, V. A. Golyashov, V. L. Alperovich, and O. E. Tereshchenko
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Fluctuation-Induced Giant Magnetoresistance in Charge-Neutral Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev

The Johnson-Nyquist noise associated with the intrinsic conductivity of the electron liquid induces fluctuations of the electron density in charge-neutral graphene devices. In the presence of external electric and magnetic fields, the fluctuations of charge density and electric current induce a fluc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126301] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev

The Johnson-Nyquist noise associated with the intrinsic conductivity of the electron liquid induces fluctuations of the electron density in charge-neutral graphene devices. In the presence of external electric and magnetic fields, the fluctuations of charge density and electric current induce a fluc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126301] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluctuation-Induced Giant Magnetoresistance in Charge-Neutral Graphene
item.dc:creator: A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Biorthogonal Neural Network Approach to 2D Non-Hermitian Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla

Non-Hermitian (NH) quantum many-body systems exhibit a rich array of physical phenomena, including NH skin effects and exceptional points, that remain largely inaccessible to existing numerical techniques. In this Letter, we investigate the application of variational Monte Carlo and neural network w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126501] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla

Non-Hermitian (NH) quantum many-body systems exhibit a rich array of physical phenomena, including NH skin effects and exceptional points, that remain largely inaccessible to existing numerical techniques. In this Letter, we investigate the application of variational Monte Carlo and neural network w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126501] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Biorthogonal Neural Network Approach to 2D Non-Hermitian Systems
item.dc:creator: Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Engineering Long-Range and Multibody Interactions via Global Kinetic Constraints
item.description:
Author(s): Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao

Long-range and multibody interactions are crucial for quantum simulation and quantum computation. Yet, their practical realization using elementary pairwise interactions remains an outstanding challenge. We propose an experimental scheme based on the Bose-Hubbard system with a periodic driving of th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao

Long-range and multibody interactions are crucial for quantum simulation and quantum computation. Yet, their practical realization using elementary pairwise interactions remains an outstanding challenge. We propose an experimental scheme based on the Bose-Hubbard system with a periodic driving of th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Engineering Long-Range and Multibody Interactions via Global Kinetic Constraints
item.dc:creator: Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Direct Raman Observation of the Quantum Metric in a Quantum Magnet
item.description:
Author(s): Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang

The quantum geometric tensor unifies the Berry curvature (its imaginary part) and the quantum metric (its real part), yet Raman studies of chiral phonons have so far accessed only the former. We perform circularly polarized Raman spectroscopy on the quantum magnet ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}\mathrm{Co}({\math…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116605] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang

The quantum geometric tensor unifies the Berry curvature (its imaginary part) and the quantum metric (its real part), yet Raman studies of chiral phonons have so far accessed only the former. We perform circularly polarized Raman spectroscopy on the quantum magnet K2Co(SeO3)2, where the field-odd ch…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116605] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Direct Raman Observation of the Quantum Metric in a Quantum Magnet
item.dc:creator: Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
